pattern: Sliding window tracking per-bit counts lets you efficiently remove the leftmost element's OR contribution and shrink the window once the OR reaches k.
hints: OR of a subarray only gains bits as you extend right; to shrink from the left you need to know which bits were contributed by the element being removed.
Keep a 30-element array counting how many elements in the current window have each bit set; the current OR is the set of bits with count > 0.
Expand right freely, then while current OR >= k shrink from the left: decrement each bit count for the removed element, update OR, and record the window length.

edge_inputs.description: k=0, any single element satisfies it
single element smaller than k, no answer
need all bits from all elements combined
large n with answer at the start
earliest pair gives OR >= k but a later single element also qualifies
k=0 but all elements are 0; OR still satisfies
no subarray can ever reach k because max OR is below k

stress.description: large random array to verify sliding window correctness
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, size)
    nums = [rng.randint(0, 10**9) for _ in range(n)]
    k = rng.randint(0, 10**9)
    return [nums, k]
